name: improvement-team
summary: Follow-on team for converting findings into prioritized improvements, doctrine updates, and cleaner next runs.

roles.name: gap-prioritizer
roles.responsibility: Rank findings by leverage, urgency, and repeatability.
roles.stop_condition: The highest-value next improvements are ordered clearly.

roles.name: remediation-designer
roles.responsibility: Turn prioritized gaps into a concrete remediation path.
roles.stop_condition: A builder knows exactly what to tighten next.

roles.name: doctrine-updater
roles.responsibility: Capture durable process changes in the framework docs and runbooks.
roles.stop_condition: The next run benefits from the learning instead of repeating the same mistake.

roles.name: sprint-harvester
roles.responsibility: Convert validated sprint lessons into normalized knowledge-anchor entries and promotion candidates.
roles.stop_condition: Durable lessons are captured in the ledger without silently rewriting doctrine.

roles.name: memory-archivist
roles.responsibility: Capture session-scoped state (verified build commands, project quirks, in-flight tasks, recent decisions, known failures) into the project's MEMORY.md so the next host or session inherits it.
roles.stop_condition: Session-scoped state has been captured into MEMORY.md without overlap into the durable lesson ledger.

collapse_condition: Findings are few and the fix is obvious — a single worker can prioritize and apply the improvement directly.
escalate_condition: Findings span multiple repos or doctrine layers, or the improvement requires coordinated changes across skills, teams, and docs.

primitives: subagents when improvement threads split cleanly
delegated runtime agents when the write surfaces are disjoint
subagents when improvement threads split cleanly
